preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes:
v1.21.26: codex API audit closure + brand-manifest discovery fix + Gitar review followups. Eight findings closed across six PRs; one cosmetic image-card bug pivoted-and-fixed mid-flight; one bonus brand-manifest discovery fix surfaced from a live user report. (1) RATELIMIT RIGOR (autocmo-core #162) closes codex audit P0 #1 (rateLimitedDo now parses Retry-After per-response, calls RecordRateLimitHit itself with the parsed value or per-platform fallback, sleeps for the parsed duration capped at 60s — Meta + TikTok wrappers' previously-unreachable post-call 429 block removed), P0 #2 (10 outbound paths wired through PreflightCheck + RecordSuccess/RecordRateLimitHit: Discord proxy, Slack OAuth channel discovery × 2 sites, Slack watchdog webhook, Gemini × 2 functions, Veo submit + every poll, GSC Search Console × 2 sites, plus 4 additional Gemini bypasses the audit missed — creative_gates / creative_judge / quality × 2 / scriptgen — caught by a new static `TestNoUnpreflightedPlatformHost` scanner that fails CI on any *.go file referencing a known platform host without a paired preflight), P1 #1 (per-platform fallback backoffs for zero-Retry-After 429s: 60s for AppLovin/Postscript/Merchant/GA4/Meta/TikTok/Foreplay/TrendTrack/google_ai/google_search_console; 30s for Slack/Discord/default), P1 #2 (Gemini API key moved from `?key=` URL form to x-goog-api-key header in seo_cluster.go's embedBatch + labelClusterViaGemini — *url.Error propagates request URLs through fmt.Errorf("%w") on transport failures, so the URL form was leaking the key into logs), and P2 #3 (ParseRetryAfter now handles RFC 7231 HTTP-date in all three formats — IMF-fixdate, RFC 850, asctime — via http.ParseTime; past dates return 0 so callers don't compute negative sleeps). (2) MCP SURFACE RIGOR (#224) closes audit P1 #3 — four new first-class MCP tools whose binary actions previously had no LLM-facing surface: `mcp__merlin__stripe` (read-only revenue/subscriptions/cohorts/analytics + setup + preference), `mcp__merlin__google_merchant` (status/setup/sync-shopify/insights), `mcp__merlin__reddit_organic` (scan + draft, idempotent reads/staging) + `mcp__merlin__reddit_organic_post` (the publish step, destructive + non-idempotent — split per Gitar review on the original PR), `mcp__merlin__trendtrack` (status + verify-key). And P2 #2 — concurrency.platform may now be a function (args)→string for video/voice tools, resolved per-call so an LLM auto-mode burst can't saturate the wrong provider's queue. video routes by provider (fal/veo/heygen/arcads each to their own slot); voice routes list-avatars to heygen, the rest to elevenlabs. (3) BRAND-REQUIRED SCHEMA FLIP (#226) closes audit P2 #1 — 19 brand-scoped tools promoted from runtime-only refusal to schema-level brandRequired:true: meta_audit, google_analytics, tiktok_ads, google_ads, amazon_ads, shopify, klaviyo, postscript, email, seo, content, video, dashboard*, threads, reddit_ads, linkedin_ads, etsy, decisions, captions* (* note: dashboard kept brandRequired:false in the final commit because its action enum spans wisdom + landing-audit which are legitimately brand-less; meta_ads similarly stays false because its enum spans setup/discover/adlib). New parity test in mcp-brand-required-parity.test.js source-scans every brandRequired:true tool to enforce no dispatched action overlaps BRAND_OPTIONAL_ACTIONS AND brand input uses brandSchema (not .optional()). (4) IMAGE-CARD UNAVAILABLE FIX (#223) — live user report mid-audit-work: `Image still appears as unavailable once rendered with the card style`. Root cause: race between the Go binary's image-pipeline write and the gallery-sentinel emission on Windows. Pre-fix the merlin:// protocol handler stat'd a 0-byte file (during atomic-write window) and returned 200 OK with empty body — browsers treated empty image responses as SUCCESSFUL loads (no `error` event), so the v1.21.23 placeholder couldn't recover. Fix: 425 Too Early on 0-byte files in main.js + retry-2× on stack-IMG error in gallery-viewer.js (600ms + 1500ms backoff) before declaring the image dead. (5) BRAND-MANIFEST DISCOVERY FIX (autocmo-core #163) — live user report: `assets/brands/ivory-ella/brand-manifest.json` exists but the binary logs "no brand-manifest.json found" on every render. Root cause: brandManifestSearchPaths' path 3 looked at `cfg.OutputDir/assets/brands/<brand>/` — but cfg.OutputDir is `<workspace>/results/`, brand assets live one level up. Pre-fix the path resolved to a directory that has never existed for any brand. Fix: prepend `..` so the path matches the convention used everywhere else in main.go; new path 4 added for cwd-rooted brand folders. New tests pin the path order AND anti-assert the buggy pre-fix path doesn't reappear. (6) GITAR FOLLOWUPS (this PR's session/gitar-fixes) — three findings on PR #224 + one on #162: reddit_organic split into reads (idempotent) + post (destructive, non-idempotent) so the framework's idempotency cache can't poison a retried failed post; concurrency resolver fail-CLOSED via _default platform when broken (was fail-OPEN — unmetered calls); stripe marked destructive:true preview:false because the 'preference' action mutates dashboard state; Slack channel discovery 429 short-circuits the parse step (was reading + unmarshaling Slack's `{ok:false,error:ratelimited}` body unnecessarily). Plus the legacy validateDefinition rule that destructive tools MUST be idempotent:true was relaxed — destructive + idempotent:false is now valid as an explicit "retries create dupes" annotation (the framework's idempotency cache gates on the flag, so false simply disables caching, which is correct for inherently non-idempotent operations like Reddit posts / single-send Klaviyo campaigns / SMS blasts). (7) FRIENDLY-RENDER SDK ede_diagnostic + terminal-state errors (#222) — live customer incident: a 5-variant creative-engine run hit an SDK stream-end hiccup, the Anthropic Agent SDK's raw `[ede_diagnostic] result_type=user last_content_type=n/a stop_reason=null` diagnostic surfaced verbatim in chat with `Retrying in 2s... (attempt 1/3)` below — three lines of SDK internals leaking to a paying user (Hard-Won Security Rule 6 violation). friendlyError now translates ede_diagnostic / error_during_execution / error_max_turns / error_max_budget_usd / error_max_structured_output_retries into actionable plain-English copy. (8) PIN SIDEBAR BUGS (#221, was already in v1.21.25 but the bugs surfaced post-release) — pinned sidebar still closed on outside click (every panel-close code path now respects body.has-pinned-X-sidebar); input bar didn't shift when sidebar pinned (CSS rewritten from per-element margin-right to padding-right on body + negative margin on titlebar so the existing margin:0 auto centering naturally re-centers within the reduced parent width). 4 new source-scan tests cover the click-outside / Escape / cross-panel-button gating + invariant that every inline panel hide is paired with setSidebarPinned(false). Tests at end of release: full Go suite (250+) green in 230s, full app suite 1042/1042 green.

v1.21.25: bundled batch — archive flicker fix + video QA badge + wisdom D1-ops throttle (60min) + pin sidebar UX + watchdog tool-aware extensions + stuck-chat dual-layer recovery + merlin-creative-engine skill rewrite + adversarial-audit followups across all six. (1) ARCHIVE FLICKER ON DELETE: user delete → optimistic card fade-out (250-300ms) raced the file watcher's loadArchive() (grid.innerHTML = '' + refetch); the watcher won and the entire grid blew away mid-fade. Fix: noteArchiveDelete() tracks the in-flight delete promise; the watcher callback skips reload while the promise is in flight + 800ms grace + a 30s hard wall-clock fallback for hung IPC. Plus debounce on the watcher (500ms) collapses Windows' multi-event sibling fires. Routed all 4 archive-context delete call sites (single-card, bulk-trash, viewer-trash, right-click context-menu) through archiveDeleteFile(). (2) VIDEO QA BADGE: pre-fix the video pipeline never set runMeta.QAPassed before metadata.json write — every successful video tile rendered ✗ QA in the archive sidebar. Fix: four-case branch at write time (failure / !qaEnabled / !finalGateRan / qaReport.Final.Pass), with a finalGateRan flag mirroring the image pipeline's imageQARan pattern so silent-video and avatar-video runs (where the merge gate intentionally skips) correctly badge ✓ QA. (3) WISDOM EAGER-AGG THROTTLE: raised 10min → 60min (EAGER_AGG_THROTTLE_MS constant). Each agg run is ~14-16 D1 ops; pre-fix ~50% of /api/report POSTs triggered one. Cuts D1 ops 30-40% on a busy day. Plus race-serialization fix: the prior SELECT-then-UPSERT didn't actually serialize concurrent isolates (both could pass throttle, both run aggregate). Replaced with conditional UPDATE + meta.changes === 1 gate so only one isolate wins. (4) PIN SIDEBAR UX: new pin button on Magic + Archive headers — click to pin → sidebar stays open AND chat reflows left by 340px (no overlay). Persists in localStorage. Boot script (app/boot-pin.js, external file because the meta CSP blocks inline <script>) sets <html data-pinned-sidebar> before first paint to prevent FOUC flicker. setSidebarPinned() mirrors body-class state to the html data-attr in BOTH directions (set on pin, clear on unpin with identity guard) so the CSS first-paint rule and the runtime body-class rule stay in sync. Mutual exclusivity: only one sidebar pinned at a time. (5) STUCK-CHAT WATCHDOG TOOL-AWARE: the v1.21.24 stuck-chat watchdog used a flat 90s timeout that interrupted legitimate long tools (brand scrape, video gen, batch image, competitor scan). New three-tier model: TEXT_STALL_MS=90s when no tool is in flight, TOOL_STALL_MS=600s (10min) rolling extension while a tool is in flight, HARD_MAX_STALL_MS=900s (15min) absolute ceiling regardless of state. Tool-flight tracked via _pendingToolCount on content_block_start with type='tool_use'. (6) MERLIN-CREATIVE-ENGINE SKILL REWRITE: the prior version emitted a verbose BRAND MODEL preamble + matrix integrity declaration + per-variant text blocks + run-summary footer to chat. Ryan's APOTHEKE 12-variant fan-out put a wall of plan text between the user and the actual creatives. New contract: ONE status line + the gallery, all planning silent. Phase 2 inverted from 'draft text brief per row' to 'compose brief internally + invoke mcp__merlin__content per variant in parallel'. Plus MANDATORY post-tool gallery-echo rule (mirrors merlin-content's) so Claude never paraphrases N gallery <div>s as a numbered file-path list. Pinned by app/pin-sidebar.test.js (13 tests), app/archive-flicker-suppression.test.js (9 tests), app/stuck-chat-watchdog.test.js (15 tests), autocmo-core/video_qa_badge_test.go (6 tests), autocmo-core/wisdom-api/worker.test.mjs (4 new throttle/race tests). Tests: 1024 JS green, 228s Go suite green, 71 worker tests green.

v1.21.25: merlin-creative-engine skill — autonomous mass creative generation with silent orchestration. Live UX feedback (2026-05-04): the prior version of the creative-engine skill emitted a verbose BRAND MODEL preamble + matrix integrity declaration + per-variant text blocks + run-summary footer to chat — Ryan's APOTHEKE 12-variant fan-out put a wall of plan text between the user and the actual creatives. New contract: Claude/Merlin handle ALL the planning silently (matrix sampling, brief drafting, EvaluateBrief audit, Copy Quality Gate, forbidden_angles veto, brand-manifest enforcement) and the chat output is a SINGLE status line ('Fanning out 12 APOTHEKE variants — generating images…') followed by the gallery of creatives. Per-variant brief detail is attached to image metadata for click-through inspection — never dumped on render. Power-user escape hatch: `--show-briefs` re-enables the verbose output for debug / brief-export use cases. Phase 2 changes from 'draft text brief per row' to 'compose brief internally + invoke mcp__merlin__content / mcp__merlin__video per variant in parallel'; the binary's emitArtifactBundle handles the gallery rendering automatically. Andromeda-friendly: `imageCount` + `varyDimension` per variant for axis rotation around strong briefs. Defaults preserved: banana-pro-edit (image), seedance-2 (video), heygen-agent (talking-head, REQUIRED for any face-in-frame). Brand-manifest auto-scaffolder (v1.21.23) + atomic image downloads (v1.21.24) make the autonomous-generate path safe — every brief carries productRefPath + compositeMode:true so banana-pro-edit composites the real product photo instead of hallucinating it. Tier C (22KB) — well under 50KB cap. 5 new routing-corpus utterances ('fan out 30 ads', 'spin up a creative pass for [brand]', 'give me 50 hooks across all our demos', 'I need 20 retargeting variants across angles', 'test a bunch of angles with 25 image briefs'). Tests: skills validator green, 998 JS green.

v1.21.24: bundled fix-up — stuck-chat watchdog + atomic image downloads + archive-thumb data-loaded gate + status-label cleanup. (0) STUCK-CHAT WATCHDOG (added late 2026-05-04 after live customer-churn incident): customer's chat froze mid-stream — model started a response ('Got 12 fresh handles. Selecting 5 with strong summer/transitional prints, fetching their back-image URLs in parallel.') with cursor blinking, and the SDK never emitted a `result`. Input stayed disabled, status pill stayed up, even switching brands didn't unstick the UI. Two failure modes, two layers of recovery: (a) For-await loop ENDS without `result` (Anthropic API closed stream mid-turn / network truncation / MCP tool returned EOF before the model finished its turn) — main.js now tracks _sawTerminalResult and synthesizes a {type:'result', subtype:'truncated', _synthetic:true} terminal so the renderer's existing result-handler path runs. The renderer appends a ⚠️ marker to the bubble so the user sees the response was incomplete instead of thinking Merlin gave up. (b) For-await loop is BLOCKED indefinitely (hung MCP tool past SDK timeout, silent network drop) — renderer-side stream watchdog: STREAM_STALL_MS=90s without any SDK event during an active turn triggers force-recovery: append the warning marker, finalize the bubble, clear sessionActive / setInputDisabled / typingIndicator / statusLabel / tickingTimer / streamWatchdog, abortActiveQuery upstream so the next send doesn't queue behind a dead turn. Plus brand-switch full-reset: paintBrandThread + preseedBrandSwitch both now clear ALL turn-state flags (sessionActive, setInputDisabled, typingIndicator, statusLabel, tickingTimer, streamWatchdog) — pre-fix only isStreaming was reset, so a hung turn could keep input disabled across a brand switch. Brand-switch is the user's escape hatch when the chat is wedged; it MUST work no matter what state the prior turn left behind. Pinned by app/stuck-chat-watchdog.test.js (10 tests). (1) ATOMIC IMAGE DOWNLOADS — root cause closure for the chopped-in-half image bug. Live incident anchor (2026-05-04, fal-image-chopped-in-half-while-downloading): user generates image via fal.ai, the binary downloads to its FINAL on-disk path while bytes stream over the wire. Renderer's directory scans (sidebar archive refresh on every brand activation + chat-gallery emit + full-screen viewer preload) read the file mid-stream; Chromium decoded what it could and painted a chopped-in-half image. Same bug class as Hard-Won Security Rule 7 (atomic-write siblings on vault writes) — the renderer must NEVER see a partial file. Fix: new atomicDownload(resp, dest) helper streams to dest+'.tmp', fsyncs the bytes to physical storage (forces durability before metadata rename), then atomic-renames to dest. Both downloadFile and downloadFileWithKey route through this single helper. Cleanup: io.Copy / fsync / close failures all remove the .tmp sibling. Pinned by 4 new tests in autocmo-core/download_atomic_test.go (chunked-response final-path-only-appears-atomically, mid-stream connection close cleans .tmp sibling, downloadFileWithKey atomic semantics + API key forwarding, source-scan that fsync runs between io.Copy and rename). (2) ARCHIVE-THUMB DATA-LOADED GATE — defense-in-depth for the sidebar. The atomic-download fix is the root cause closure (renderer never sees a partial file again), but .archive-card-thumb IMGs in the sidebar were missing the v1.21.16-style data-loaded gate that .merlin-artifact img has. Mirror the chat-gallery hook: opacity:0 until renderer.js's wireArchiveThumbLoadGate flips data-loaded='true' on load (img.complete + naturalWidth>0 fast-path for cache hits, addEventListener('load') for fresh fetches, addEventListener('error') for fall-through). Wired on both archive grid render paths (regular archive list + Live Ads grid). (3) STATUS-LABEL CLEANUP — drop the '— taking a while...' stuck-suffix per Ryan: it added visual noise on legitimately-long tools (image / video / voice generation, brand scrape, large ad batches, HeyGen renders) and the Cancel-button fade-in at 30s is the real escape hatch for hung calls. Status now stays exactly as the tool first set it (e.g. 'BREWING AN IMAGE' through the entire long render). Removed: the 45s setTimeout in setStatusLabel that mutated labelEl.textContent. Preserved: Cancel-button fade-in at CANCEL_BTN_DELAY_MS, per-tool labels, abort-active-query IPC handler. Tests: 228s Go suite green (was 224s; +4s from 4 new atomic-download tests), 988 JS green.

v1.21.23: bundled fix-up release — DPA catalog edge-probe + Gitar findings cleanup + brand-manifest auto-scaffolder + image-card error UX. (1) DPA CATALOG EDGE-PROBE: meta_dpa_setup catalog validation gains a third fallback path — products/product_sets-edge probe — for catalogs reachable to the user but not in any /me/businesses owned-list. Live incident anchor (2026-05-04, dpa-catalog-not-in-owned-list): post-v1.21.22 the catalog validation routed through business-scoped lookup correctly but Ryan's catalog 254040002595373 (Ivory Ella) is reachable for ad targeting WITHOUT appearing in any business he can list via /me/businesses → owned_product_catalogs (real Meta ownership pattern: partner-shared / client-granted / non-direct-member). Fix: add path 3 — when paths 1+2 miss, probe `GET /<catalogID>/product_sets?fields=id&limit=1`. Same edge runMetaDPASetup hits in step 2, so success here means the next step is guaranteed to work too. On probe failure surface Meta's error_user_msg verbatim. Pinned by 2 new tests in meta_dpa_setup_test.go. (2) GITAR FINDINGS CLEANUP: address all 4 actionable findings on PRs #150/#151/#152 — meta_dpa_setup.go hardcoded US-only geo→cfg.getTargetCountries() (real bug for non-US brands; matches every other Meta ad-creation flow), klaviyo_templates_actions.go HTTP 5 substring→Klaviyo-prefix-anchored match (no more body false-positives), meta_pixel_discovery_test.go bounds-clamp on slice (panic prevention), meta.go brandMatches normalize-first guards (semantic clarity). (3) BRAND-MANIFEST AUTO-SCAFFOLDER: closes the producer/consumer gap on assets/brands/<brand>/brand-manifest.json — autocmo-core/brand_manifest.go has shipped the consumer-side enforcement contract since v1.21.5+, but onboarding (merlin-setup), product import, and brand activation all shipped without ever creating one. Result: every brand on Merlin (POG, MadChill, Merlin, example, ivory-ella, every future brand) sat in the no-manifest soft-warn path; mcp__merlin__content fell back to non-composite mode; banana-pro-edit treated references/1.jpg as a style hint and re-rendered the garment from scratch instead of preserving it. Wrong product, every time. Fix: new app/brand-manifest-scaffolder.js — called inside ctx.activateBrand (host-side, the merlin-setup skill invokes brand_activate the instant brand.md exists) — walks products/<slug>/references/*.{jpg,jpeg,png,webp} and logo/logo.* and emits a brand-manifest.json with paths relative to the manifest dir. Idempotent: hand-curated manifests with extra fields (visual_direction, voice_rules, compliance) survive untouched; rebuild:true forces regeneration; 0-byte stub files are treated as 'no manifest'; brands with zero canonical assets skip and retry on next brand_activate. Pinned by app/brand-manifest-scaffolder.test.js (16 tests covering deriveDisplayName, full-scaffold happy path, hand-curated manifest preservation, rebuild flag, 0-byte stub recovery, empty-brand refusal, logo-only brands, invalid-slug guards, missing-directory guard, cross-platform path encoding, plus listRefImages/findBrandLogo/listProductSlugs unit tests). (4) IMAGE-CARD ERROR UX: stack IMG load-state gating + friendly fallback tile. Pre-fix when an IMG src failed to resolve (404 from merlin:// handler, missing/0-byte file from a partial pipeline write), Chromium rendered its tiny broken-icon glyph in the top-left of the card on a transparent background — user saw a dark card with a colored triangle and no signal about what went wrong (Ryan's screenshot showed exactly this). Fix: gallery-viewer.js sets data-load-state='pending' on creation; load handler flips to 'loaded' (CSS opacity:0→1 fade); error handler swaps the IMG out for a .merlin-stack-fallback tile with 'Image unavailable' text + hover-tooltip carrying the failed URL. CSS gate keeps the IMG transparent during the request window so the broken-icon glyph never paints. Tests: 988 JS green (was 972; +16 from scaffolder tests).

v1.21.22: meta_dpa_setup catalog validation routes through business-scoped lookup — products/product_sets-edge reachability probe — for catalogs reachable to the user but NOT in any /me/businesses owned-list. Live incident anchor (2026-05-04, dpa-catalog-not-in-owned-list): post-v1.21.22 the catalog validation routed through business-scoped lookup correctly, but Ryan's catalog 254040002595373 (Ivory Ella) is reachable for ad targeting WITHOUT appearing in any business he can list via /me/businesses → owned_product_catalogs. This is a real Meta ownership pattern: catalogs can be partner-shared (Business Manager → 'Add People & Assets'), client-granted (client_product_catalogs), or owned by a business the user can target ads to but isn't a direct member of. `meta_ads action=catalog` worked for him because that path went straight to GET /<catalogID>/products when he supplied the id explicitly — same edge endpoint family, same business_management scope, but it doesn't require the catalog to appear in any owned-list. Fix: add a third path to metaResolveCatalogViaBusiness — when paths 1+2 (ad account → business owned-catalogs, /me/businesses scan) both miss, probe `GET /<catalogID>/product_sets?fields=id&limit=1`. That's the SAME edge runMetaDPASetup hits in step 2 (metaResolveAllProductsSet), so success here means the next step is guaranteed to work too. On probe failure we surface Meta's error_user_msg verbatim so the user sees exactly which permission boundary they hit. The placeholder name '(catalog <id>)' returned when the probe path is taken is acceptable degradation — the id is what gets used downstream anyway, and only the human-readable name was lost; the id matches what the user supplied. Pinned by 2 new tests in autocmo-core/meta_dpa_setup_test.go: TestMetaResolveCatalogViaBusiness_FallsBackToProductSetsProbe (httptest mock: paths 1+2 return empty owned-lists, path 3 product_sets edge returns 200; asserts probe was hit exactly once and the catalog id is echoed in the placeholder name) and TestMetaResolveCatalogViaBusiness_ProbeFailureSurfacesMetaError (probe returns 404 with Meta's error_user_title + error_user_msg payload; asserts the user-facing error contains the verbatim error_user_msg AND points at meta_ads action=catalog for self-recovery). Tests: 229s Go suite green.

v1.21.22: meta_dpa_setup catalog validation routes through business-scoped lookup — fixes persistent NOT_FOUND for users without catalog_management OAuth scope. Live incident anchor (2026-05-04, dpa-catalog-not-found-on-direct-GET): post-v1.21.21 the brand-match was correct (Ivory Ella page + pixel both stamped to the brand vault) but meta_dpa_setup STILL returned NOT_FOUND on the bare-minimum preview payload. Root cause: Meta's Marketing API has asymmetric scope rules — `GET /<catalog_id>` requires the `catalog_management` permission, but `GET /<business_id>/owned_product_catalogs` (which `meta_ads action=catalog` uses) only needs `business_management`. Merlin's Meta OAuth scope is `ads_management,pages_manage_ads,pages_read_engagement,business_management,pages_show_list` — `catalog_management` was never requested (never needed for ads/insights/audiences), so the direct catalog GET in v1.21.20's runMetaDPASetup hit NOT_FOUND for every user — which is why Ryan saw the failure even after v1.21.21's brand-match fix. Fix: replace the direct `metaGetStrict('/<catalogID>?fields=id,name,product_count')` with a new `metaResolveCatalogViaBusiness(cfg, catalogID)` helper that mirrors the business-scoped discovery pattern from runMetaCatalog (meta.go:4135-4310): (1) fast path — ad account → owning business → owned_product_catalogs list → match by id; (2) fallback — `/me/businesses` scan → for each → owned_product_catalogs list → match by id. Both endpoints only need `business_management` (which IS in scope). The catalog metadata returned (name, product_count) is the same as the direct GET would have surfaced. On a complete miss across both paths, the helper returns an actionable error pointing the user at `meta_ads action=catalog` for self-recovery — same self-recovery pattern v1.21.18 introduced for missing pixels / retargeting campaigns. The downstream calls in meta_dpa_setup (product_sets list/create, audience validation, ad set creation with promoted_object) all use catalog-edge endpoints that ALREADY work with `business_management` — only the direct catalog GET was the blocker. No re-OAuth required, no Meta App Review re-cycle. Pinned by autocmo-core/meta_dpa_setup_test.go: 5 new tests (ResolvesViaAdAccountBusiness — fast path; FallsBackToMeBusinessesScan — fallback path; NotFoundReturnsActionableError — error message points at meta_ads action=catalog; RejectsEmpty — input validation; RunMetaDPASetupNoLongerUsesDirectCatalogGet — source-scan that fails CI if any future cleanup sweep reverts to direct-GET). Tests: 224s Go suite green.

v1.21.21: brandMatches separator-normalization + loud-warn on silent fall-through. Live incident anchor (2026-05-04, ivory-ella-page-picked-madchill): brand 'ivory-ella' with two Meta pages on the same access token — 'Ivory Ella' (the right one) and 'madchill' (wrong) — saw the wrong page silently stamped into the per-brand vault. v1.21.20's meta_dpa_setup landed but was unusable for the ivory-ella user because the catalog GET ran in business scope tied to the wrong page. Root cause: brandMatches('Ivory Ella', 'ivory-ella') returned false because strings.Contains('ivory ella', 'ivory-ella') is false (space vs hyphen). The page-picker fell through to pages.Data[0] = madchill. Fix part 1: brandMatches now normalizes the four separators Meta + Merlin slugs + ad-network display names use interchangeably (space, hyphen, underscore, dot) before substring/exact-match comparison — 'Ivory Ella' / 'ivory-ella' / 'ivory_ella' / 'Ivory.Ella' all collapse to 'ivoryella' and match. Fix part 2: when brandName is set AND multiple items exist AND brandMatches found nothing, the page / ad account / pixel pickers in runMetaLogin now print a 3-line WARN block listing all available items with IDs and pointing the user at meta_setup_account — pre-fix the silent fall-through to Data[0] only became visible when downstream catalog/DPA calls failed with NOT_FOUND in the wrong-business scope. This pattern is one matcher (brandMatches) used by every brand-keyed picker in meta.go (ad accounts, pages, pixels, catalogs) and analytics.go (GA4 properties), so one fix repairs every brand-discovery path. Pinned by autocmo-core/meta_brand_match_test.go (13 tests covering separator-normalization across all four separator pairs, the 3-char minimum-substring guard, the 'madchill should NOT match ivory-ella' negative case, and source-scans pinning the loud-warn fragments for all three pickers). The v1.21.17-anchored TestRunMetaLoginPixelBrandMatch test had its scan-window expanded 1500→2500 chars to accommodate the new loud-warn block (the assertions about brandMatches usage + .ID/.Name assignment are unchanged). Tests: 218s Go suite green.

v1.21.20: meta_dpa_setup — full catalog DPA retargeting tool surface. Closes the capability gap surfaced by the live ivory-ella incident (2026-05-04). Pre-existing retargeting tools (meta_prepare_retargeting, meta_promote_to_retargeting, meta-setup-retargeting) build a custom-audience around a single source ad — they do NOT accept catalog ID, product set ID, custom audience include/exclude arrays, frequency cap, attribution-window override, or {{product.*}} template variables. v1.21.20 ships the missing capability end-to-end: new MCP tool mcp__merlin__meta_dpa_setup with the full DPA parameter surface (catalogId, productSetId, includeAudienceIds[], excludeAudienceIds[], frequencyCapEvents/Days, attributionClickDays/ViewDays, dpaHeadline/dpaPrimaryText/dpaDescription/dpaCallToAction with {{product.*}} placeholder grammar, dailyBudget, campaignId/campaignName); new binary action meta-dpa-setup wired through main.go's router; new runMetaDPASetup handler in meta_dpa_setup.go (~280 LOC) that validates the catalog (Meta /<catalogId>?fields=id,name,product_count), resolves or creates the product set (idempotent — re-runs return the same id for 'Merlin - All Products'), validates each audience id via metaGetStrict (surfaces Meta's error_user_msg verbatim — a deleted audience id surfaces as 'Custom Audience X cannot be loaded'), applies sensible defaults (3/7d freq, 7d-click + 1d-view attribution, SHOP_NOW CTA, '{{product.name | titleize}}' headline), resolves target campaign (defaults to the auto-created Retargeting campaign; honors cmd.CampaignID > cmd.CampaignName overrides), validates the daily budget against cfg.MaxDailyAdBudget, then creates the ad set with full DPA targeting (custom_audiences + excluded_custom_audiences + product_audience_specs + frequency_control_specs + attribution_spec + promoted_object), creates the dynamic carousel creative with object_story_spec.template_data + format_option=carousel_images_multi_items, and creates the ad — all in PAUSED status (explicit-activation guards spend; flip via meta_activate_asset or Ads Manager). Also added: meta_dpa_setup → meta-dpa-setup mapping in INTENT_TOOL_TO_ACTION (mcp-approval-policy.js) so the spend-approval card fires before ad-set creation. Pinned by autocmo-core/meta_dpa_setup_test.go (9 tests covering Command struct fields, action router wiring, preflight checks, PAUSED-status guard, defaults, targeting shape, templated creative, idempotent product-set resolver, and audience-id validation). Tests: 218s Go suite green, 1057 JS green (was 1056; +1 from the auto-cross-check that flagged meta_dpa_setup needs an entry in INTENT_TOOL_TO_ACTION).

v1.21.19: Klaviyo template-update resilience — single-call 429 retry + friendlier error messages. Two coordinated fixes from a live SaaS-readiness audit (2026-05-04). (1) klaviyoUpdateTemplateWithRetry — bulk-CREATE has retried inline since the v1.21.x POG 27-flow incident; UPDATE was bare-call only, so a single 429 in a multi-template loop forced the caller to re-invoke. New helper mirrors the create-with-retry pattern (single 35s sleep + one retry), wired into runKlaviyoTemplateUpdate. (2) friendlyKlaviyoError wraps the raw 'Klaviyo API HTTP <code>: <body>' format with one-line actionable remediation: 404 → 'template not found / wrong account', 401/403 → 're-run klaviyo-login (lacks templates:write)', 429-after-retry → 'pause ~60s before retrying', 5xx → 'transient, retry in a minute'. Unknown shapes pass through verbatim so we never silently hide unfamiliar failures. Pinned by autocmo-core/klaviyo_template_resilience_test.go (9 tests). Audit verdict — public mcp__merlin__klaviyo template-update path is verified end-to-end safe for the 51-template bulk loop and for re-pushing the 4 already-IPC-pushed templates (Klaviyo PATCH is idempotent, ~24s for 4 with rate-limit spacing, no duplicate templates, no flow detachment). Wave C scanForbiddenBrandWords + scanHallucinatedStat gates fire BEFORE the API call so re-running drift-out-of-policy content correctly refuses with `force=true` override. Audit also surfaced two follow-up items not shipping in this release: missing templates:read templates:write in Klaviyo OAuth scopes (works today via API key, will 403 when Phase 2 OAuth ships); no native bulk-update action (51-template loop is the canonical pattern today). Tests: 218s Go suite green.

v1.21.18: Meta retargeting NOT_FOUND — surfaced + fixed at the root. Three coordinated fixes for the live user incident where every retargeting endpoint (meta-retarget / meta-setup-retargeting / meta_prepare_retargeting) blanket-failed with NOT_FOUND for ivory-ella while insights + audits worked fine. (1) Pixel brand-match in runMetaLogin — pre-fix this blindly picked pixels.Data[0] regardless of cmd.Brand, so multi-brand-on-one-account users had the wrong pixel id stamped into their per-brand vault at OAuth time. v1.21.16 fixed this for pages + ad accounts but missed pixels — the very resource every retargeting custom-audience and DPA path depends on. Mirror the page brand-match pattern: when cmd.Brand is non-empty, fuzzy-match via brandMatches() against pixel names; fall back to Data[0] only when no name match. (2) metaGetStrict helper — read-then-error wrapper that surfaces Meta's error_user_title + error_user_msg on non-2xx GETs. metaCopyWinnerToRetargeting (meta.go:431) and metaDuplicateAd (meta.go:1620) previously called metaGet then unmarshalled into a Creative struct, saw Creative.ID=='' (because the body was actually {error:{code:100,error_user_msg:…}}), and returned the generic 'cannot read creative from ad %s' — the field that says WHICH resource is missing was silently discarded. Both retargeting + duplicate paths now route through metaGetStrict so users see Meta's error_user_msg verbatim. v1.21.12 wired metaErrorPreview into metaPost / image-upload / video-upload / batch but never the metaGet-then-unmarshal path; this closes that gap. (3) Actionable error messages on missing prerequisites — 'metaPixelId is empty' now points at meta-discover (re-runs page/pixel/ad-account auto-discovery without re-OAuth and writes per-brand vault); 'retargeting campaign not found' now points at meta-setup-retargeting (creates audiences) → meta-setup (creates campaign trio). Users self-recover from a known config gap without source-code archeology. Pinned by autocmo-core/meta_pixel_discovery_test.go (5 tests covering the strict-helper contract, retargeting paths' migration, brand-match pattern, and actionable error messages). Tests: 218s Go suite green. 

v1.21.17: Mac brand-persistence RSI — fixes the live incident where Mac users were re-routed into brand setup on every relaunch. Three reinforcing root causes addressed in one patch. (1) `.merlin-state.json` (which holds activeBrand) was written ONLY to ContentDir (~/Merlin) — a folder some users moved, renamed, had nuked by macOS Optimize Storage, or excluded from backups. New REDUNDANT writes: every state mutation lands in BOTH ContentDir AND StateDir (~/Library/Application Support/Merlin), with reads merging by `_ts` timestamp. Either alone is sufficient to skip re-onboarding. (2) Brand-existence scan only checked one path; misses brands stranded in legacy paths (~/Documents/Merlin from pre-1.0 installs, ~/Library/Application Support/Merlin from pre-D1-split installs, or wherever `cwd` resolved differently for the binary on translocated DMG launches). New `discoverBrands()` scans every known path canonical-first, AND auto-recovers stranded brands by copying them to the canonical location so subsequent launches find them on the fast path. (3) `isFirstRun` had a single signal — `CLAUDE.md` existence in ContentDir. Bootstrap was async (`setTimeout(bootstrapWorkspace, 500)` after window paint), so a fast-clicking user OR a translocated DMG with a read-only source path that silently failed `cp -Rn` could leave CLAUDE.md missing — and EVERY relaunch then mis-classified as a fresh install. New `isReturningUser()` checks FIVE independent signals (state file, CLAUDE.md, brand discovery, vault, config). False (true first-run) requires ALL five missing. Bootstrap is now SYNCHRONOUS-and-VERIFIED via `fs.cpSync` (Node-native, no platform-specific cp/robocopy callback chain), runs BEFORE createWindow so the ready-to-show handler can never race it, and verifies CLAUDE.md landed post-copy with a loud `appendErrorLog` on failure. Also: `.merlin-state.json` and `.merlin-threads.json` added to `version.json` preserve list so `/update` can't vaporize chat bubble cache or activeBrand. Three append-only logs (.merlin-errors.log, activity.jsonl, .merlin-audit.log) now route through unified rotating helpers — every appendFileSync outside `appendErrorLog` / `appendActivityLog` / `appendAudit` is a CI failure (source-scan in app/log-rotation.test.js). Tests: app/mac-persistence.test.js (15 new), app/log-rotation.test.js (5 new), all 1056 existing JS tests still green. Skip-this-version-if: you're not on Mac AND you've never reported this issue (Win/Linux unchanged in observable behavior; the redundancy + multi-path scan are pure-additive).

v1.21.16: Cancel UX + chat gallery + Meta integration completeness — five coordinated fixes responding to live user reports. (1) Escape-cancel now actually stops the SDK turn. Pre-fix it called only `merlin.stopGeneration()` which signaled the next-turn generator without aborting the in-flight query, so late text_delta events kept arriving, opened a stale 'half' bubble, and that bubble absorbed the NEXT user turn's tokens — the two-conversations-in-one-bubble bug. Now Escape calls `merlin.abortActiveQuery()` (the real interrupt), marks the live bubble dataset.canceled='true' so any racing tokens drop, calls `clearStatusLabel()` so the 'TALKING TO META' pill clears, and content_block_start opens a fresh bubble when prior was canceled. (2) Chat-gallery partial-render v2: dropped `background:#000` from `.merlin-artifact img/video` (same trap as v1.21.14's gallery-viewer fix), gated IMG opacity behind a data-loaded='true' flag, and added a shimmer ::before that sits in place during the load window. The renderer's gallery hook attaches load+error listeners that flip the flag. Pre-fix slow CDN responses left half-decoded scanlines stuck in the chat with a sharp black band where the rest should have been. (3) Streaming prefix cache reuses cached HTML across frames — pre-fix it stored prefixHtml but never read it, re-parsing the full prefix every frame (DOMPurify ran over a ~5KB dashboard reply 30-60× per second). Fix: extend cached prefix only with the delta since the previous frame, ~80% less markdown+sanitize work per frame on long streams. (4) Meta integration completeness — bulk-push and single-ad push now honor `cmd.CampaignID` and `cmd.CampaignName` via a new `resolveTargetCampaign` helper (was forced to auto-create a fresh Testing campaign every time). meta_launch_test_batch + meta_launch_test_ad zod schemas now accept campaignId. meta_scale_winner now routes to `meta-duplicate` (was silently no-op-routing to `meta-warmup` which is the API-permissions-ladder action — ad spend went nowhere). Image upload, video upload, and batch sub-response errors now route through `metaErrorPreview` so error_user_title + error_user_msg get surfaced (was raw body / 200-char truncation; sibling miss from the v1.21.12 NOT_FOUND opacity fix). (5) runMetaLogin now accepts *Command and brand-matches pages + ad accounts — pre-fix it blindly picked accts.Data[0]/pages.Data[0], so users with multiple Meta pages on one account had the wrong page stamped into their per-brand vault scope at OAuth time. Every subsequent push for the wrong-paged brand returned NOT_FOUND on downstream resources (catalog/pixel/audience that belonged to the OTHER page). Fix: when cmd.Brand is non-empty, fuzzy-match via brandMatches() against AllAccounts + AllPages and prefer those over Data[0]. Falls back to Data[0] when no name match (legacy behavior). Refine flow now emits artifact bundle so the chat gallery renders; merlin-content SKILL adds a 'Mandatory post-tool: echo gallery block verbatim' instruction so Claude stops paraphrasing variants as inline backticked paths. Pinned by app/v1-21-16-bundle.test.js (14 tests). Tests: 219s Go suite green, 1036 JS green (was 1022; +14 new). Bundles v1.21.15 Mac DMG-staple fix (CI-only) and earlier tonight's fixes back to v1.21.11.
whatsNew: Brand-manifest discovery is fixed. If you had `assets/brands/<brand>/brand-manifest.json` checked in, the binary logged `no brand-manifest.json found` on every render and proceeded WITHOUT the canonical-asset safety gate. Renders happened to come out right only when productRefPath was passed explicitly — the gate was silently bypassed. The path-lookup was missing a `..` hop (cfg.OutputDir is `<workspace>/results/`, the brand folder lives one level up). Fixed at the root and locked with a regression test that anti-asserts the buggy path can't come back.

Generated images no longer flash `Image unavailable` cards on Windows. There was a producer-side write race where the binary emitted the gallery sentinel slightly before the bytes hit disk — Chromium hit the merlin:// protocol on a briefly-0-byte file and treated the empty response as a SUCCESSFUL image load (no error event), so the v1.21.23 fallback placeholder couldn't recover. Fix: 0-byte files now return 425 Too Early so the browser fires `error`, and the stack-IMG error handler retries 2× with 600ms + 1500ms backoff before declaring the image dead. Permanent 404s still hit the friendly placeholder, just 2.1s later.

SDK errors no longer leak raw diagnostics to chat. If the Anthropic Agent SDK's stream ends in an unexpected state mid-creative-run (a tool returns but the assistant doesn't follow up, transient model hiccup, internal abort), you previously saw `Claude Code returned an error result: [ede_diagnostic] result_type=user last_content_type=n/a stop_reason=null` followed by `Retrying in 2s... (attempt 1/3)` — three lines of SDK internals to a paying user. friendlyError now translates ede_diagnostic + error_max_turns + error_max_budget_usd + error_max_structured_output_retries into actionable plain-English copy. The retry loop is unchanged (it works correctly for these cases); only the user-facing wording got fixed.

Pinned sidebars actually stay pinned now. Pre-fix, clicking anywhere outside a pinned Magic or Archive sidebar still closed it (Escape did too) — the pin button was cosmetic. Worse, hiding the panel without clearing the body class left the chat reflowed around an invisible 340px void. Now every panel-close code path checks `body.has-pinned-X-sidebar` and bails if set. Plus the input bar finally re-centers within the visible chat area when a sidebar is pinned — the prior CSS used per-element margin-right which fought with the input bar's existing `margin: 0 auto`; the new approach uses `padding-right: 340px` on body so the children's auto-centering correctly recomputes within the reduced width.

Three new MCP tools shipped. `mcp__merlin__stripe` (revenue, subscriptions, cohorts, analytics, setup, preference — read-only OAuth scope is pinned in BOTH the binary and the BFF, so even though the tool is now flagged destructive for the preference action, it cannot fire any Stripe write call). `mcp__merlin__google_merchant` (status, setup, sync-shopify, insights — closes the LLM gap for `fix my Shopping ads disapprovals` queries). `mcp__merlin__reddit_organic` (scan + draft, idempotent reads/staging) + `mcp__merlin__reddit_organic_post` (the publish step, split into its own tool because it's inherently non-idempotent). `mcp__merlin__trendtrack` (status + verify-key — broader query surface ships when the matching binary actions land).

Rate-limit hygiene across every connector got tightened. Meta + TikTok 429 retries now honor the platform's Retry-After header (was a fixed 2/4/8/16/32s exponential schedule that ignored the platform's signal); every 429 contributes to the persisted preflight backoff state (was silently absorbed in the retry loop); 10 outbound paths previously bypassing the limiter (Discord proxy, Slack OAuth + watchdog, Gemini × 4 functions, Veo submit + every poll, Search Console × 2 sites, plus 4 Gemini-using files the original audit missed) are all now wired through PreflightCheck. New static `TestNoUnpreflightedPlatformHost` scanner blocks any future *.go file that references a known platform host without a paired preflight from passing CI.

Generation tools route to the correct provider's concurrency slot. video/voice tools span multiple providers (fal/veo/heygen/arcads for video; elevenlabs/heygen for voice) but pre-fix every video gen routed through fal's slot regardless of which provider actually handled the call — so a 25-video heygen burst would queue against fal's slot budget instead of heygen's. concurrency.platform now accepts a function for these tools, resolved at call time from the provider arg.

Brand-scoped MCP tools now declare brand-required at the schema level. 19 tools promoted (meta_audit, google_analytics, tiktok_ads, google_ads, amazon_ads, shopify, klaviyo, postscript, email, seo, content, video, threads, reddit_ads, linkedin_ads, etsy, decisions, plus the four new tools mentioned above). MCP clients (Claude Desktop, Codex, etc.) now see `brand` as a required field in the tool's JSON schema — confused agents get a clean Zod validation error early instead of a runtime refusal late. meta_ads + dashboard intentionally stay brandRequired:false because their action enum spans both brand-required (push/insights/kill) and brand-optional (setup/discover/adlib for meta_ads; wisdom/landing-audit for dashboard) actions; per-action enforcement still lives in runBinary's BRAND_OPTIONAL_ACTIONS gate.

Gemini API key no longer rides in URL query strings on the SEO-cluster path. *url.Error propagates request URLs through fmt.Errorf(`%w`) on transport failures (DNS, TLS, connection refused), so a key in `?key=...` would have leaked into any user-visible transport error. Moved to the x-goog-api-key header (matches the existing convention used everywhere else in the binary).

Plus from v1.21.25: archive sidebar no longer flickers when you delete a tile (file watcher debounce + in-flight delete grace), video tiles in archive show the right QA badge (✓ for passes, ✗ for failures, no more inverted), wisdom-API D1 ops cut 30-40%/day (eager-agg throttle 10min→60min), pin sidebar UX, stuck-chat watchdog tool-aware extensions, stuck-chat dual-layer recovery, merlin-creative-engine skill rewrite (silent orchestration → ONE status line + the gallery).
